senses.definition: to make a picture of something using a pencil, pen, etc. without coloring it

examples.x: He drew a cute cat on the paper for his little sister.
My little brother is able to draw a perfect circle freehand.
The artist can draw realistic portraits of people.
The child used crayons to draw a colorful butterfly.
They drew the outline of a house in their art project.

senses.definition: to produce a picture or design with paint

examples.x: She painted a beautiful landscape of the countryside.
The artist painted a portrait of his wife using oil colors.
He enjoys painting abstract art, using bold colors and shapes.
The students painted murals on the classroom walls as part of a school project.
She painted a still life of fruits and flowers for the art exhibition.

senses.definition: to produce an elementary and quick drawing of someone or something
to carefully copy or reproduce a design or image by following its lines or contours onto another surface, typically using transparent sheet

examples.x: She traced the drawing onto a new piece of paper using a pencil.
She used a transparent sheet to trace the design for her project.
The student traced the shapes to practice drawing more accurately.
He traced the outline of the map to create a new version.
She traced the contours of the landscape to add detail to her artwork.

senses.definition: to create a basic, initial version that outlines the main features of something

examples.x: The artist decided to rough out the sketch before adding the details.
The architect had to rough out the building's layout to plan its design.
The composer began to rough out the melody for the new song.
The director needed to rough out the scenes for the upcoming movie.

senses.definition: to cut or carve designs or writings on a hard surface, often using acid or a laser beam

examples.x: The artist etched a detailed illustration on a metal plate.
The jeweler etched intricate patterns onto the silver pendant.
The printmaker used a technique to etch fine lines onto the copper plate.
The scientist etched a microscopic circuit on a silicon wafer.
The glass artist etched a beautiful design onto the transparent surface.

senses.definition: to darken part of a picture or drawing using pencils, etc.

examples.x: She carefully shaded the apple in her drawing, using different pencil pressures to create depth and realism.
The art teacher demonstrated how to shade a sphere, explaining how light and shadow work together to create a three-dimensional effect.
He shaded the background of his sketch to make the foreground figures stand out more prominently.
The artist used cross-hatching techniques to shade the portrait, adding texture and dimension to the subject's face.
After outlining the tree, she began to shade the leaves, giving them a sense of volume and form.

senses.definition: to use pictures in a book, magazine, etc.

examples.x: She illustrates her articles with hand-drawn sketches.
He illustrates the cookbook with colorful pictures of the finished dishes.
They illustrate the travel guidebook with maps and photographs of landmarks.

senses.definition: to draw or color something using a pencil or stick made of colored wax or chalk
to aimlessly draw lines and shapes, particularly when one is bored

examples.x: During the meeting, he doodled on his notepad to pass the time.
She doodles in the margins of her notebook during class.
The student doodles on the back of his exam paper while waiting for time to be up.
Whenever she's on the phone, she doodles on a scrap of paper.
They doodle on napkins while waiting for their food to arrive at the restaurant.

senses.definition: to develop something using certain facts, ideas, situations, etc.

examples.x: The report is based on extensive research in the field.
The decision was based on careful consideration of the options.

senses.definition: to combine different substances together

examples.x: The chef blended various spices to create a flavorful sauce.
The artist skillfully blended colors on the canvas for a harmonious effect.
To achieve the perfect aroma, the perfumer blended different scents.
The bartender blended ingredients to craft a delicious cocktail.

senses.definition: to create or produce something by cutting or shaping a material, such as a sculpture or design

examples.x: The sculptor carved a magnificent statue from a block of marble.
The ice sculptor carved a stunning ice sculpture for the winter festival.
The artist carved a detailed sculpture from a block of wood.
The sculptor carved a marble statue that showcased the human form.

senses.definition: to draw a line around something

examples.x: In their study, participants were told to circumscribe objects on paper without looking at what they were drawing.
Students were asked to circumscribe several shapes on their paper without lifting their pencils.
Police initially circumscribed the crime scene with yellow tape to cordon off the area.
She circumscribed the area on the map to highlight the restricted zone.
The coach circumscribed a section of the field for practice drills.

senses.definition: to add something that enhances or improves the quality or appearance of someone or something

examples.x: The new accessories complemented her outfit, adding a touch of elegance.
A well-chosen background music can complement the atmosphere of a social event.
The chef carefully selected a wine to complement the flavors of the dish.
The additional software features were designed to complement the existing functionality of the application.
The interior designer used contrasting colors to complement the overall aesthetic of the room.

senses.definition: to create a representation of something, usually in the form of a drawing, painting, or other visual medium

examples.x: The artist rendered the majestic landscape with vibrant colors and intricate detail.
Using digital tools, the graphic designer rendered a lifelike illustration of the company's new product.
The sculptor meticulously rendered the human form in marble, capturing every contour and expression.
The graphic novel artist rendered scenes from the historical event with emotional depth and accuracy.
Using watercolors, the landscape painter rendered the tranquil scene of the countryside with soft hues and delicate brushstrokes.

senses.definition: to show someone or something in a piece of art

examples.x: The artist chose to represent the beauty of nature by painting a vibrant landscape filled with lush greenery and colorful flowers.
In his sculpture, the artist skillfully represented the human form.
The historical painting expertly represents the pivotal moment in the nation's history.
The abstract painting represents the artist's emotions with bold strokes and vibrant colors.

senses.definition: to repair a work of art, building, etc. so that it is in a good condition again

examples.x: The museum hired experts to restore the ancient painting to its original beauty.
They restored the historic building to its former grandeur, preserving its architectural details.
The craftsmen carefully restored the antique furniture, removing scratches and restoring the wood's luster.
After the fire, they worked tirelessly to restore the damaged house to its former glory.
The team worked for months to restore the old cathedral’s damaged windows.

senses.definition: to write or draw something in an aimless or careless way

examples.x: During the phone call, she absentmindedly scribbled on a notepad, creating intricate patterns.
He found himself scribbling on the margins of his notebook during the long lecture, letting his mind wander.
While waiting for the meeting to start, she began to scribble on a napkin, creating a series of interconnected shapes.
The artist often sits in the park, scribbling in his sketchbook, allowing inspiration to come naturally.
Unable to focus on the task at hand, he picked up a pen and began to scribble aimlessly on a piece of scrap paper.

senses.definition: to make a dirty mark by rubbing or spreading something on a surface

examples.x: She accidentally smudged her lipstick while applying it.
The painter smudged the colors together to create a softer effect.
His hands were smudged with ink after working on the drawing.
Be careful not to smudge the fresh paint on the walls.
The makeup artist gently smudged the eyeliner for a smoky eye look.

senses.definition: to use small dots or markings to create a pattern or texture often to create shading and to add detail to a work of art
to mark with spots or blotches of different color or shades of color as if stained
to skillfully make something, particularly with the hands

examples.x: She crafts handmade jewelry, carefully assembling each piece with precision.
Artisans craft intricate wooden furniture using traditional woodworking techniques.
Artists often craft sculptures from various materials, expressing their creativity.
During the holiday season, families gather to craft homemade decorations and ornaments.

senses.definition: to bring something into existence or make something happen

examples.x: Many entrepreneurs aspire to create successful businesses.
She created a beautiful painting with vibrant colors.
The scientists created a groundbreaking vaccine for the disease.
They created a new software program to solve the problem.
The artist decided to create a sculpture from marble.

senses.definition: to form a pattern or movement of lines or objects that cross each other in a regular pattern
to spread a sticky substance such as mud, paint, etc. on a surface in a careless way

examples.x: She daubed ointment on the wound to soothe the irritation and promote healing.
He daubed mud on his face as camouflage for the outdoor game.
She daubed sunscreen on her skin before heading to the beach, leaving streaks of white residue.
The chef daubed sauce onto the plate, creating an artistic presentation of the dish.
They daubed adhesive onto the back of the wallpaper before applying it to the wall.

senses.definition: to draw or trace lines on a surface

examples.x: In the drawing class, students were taught how to delineate shadows to add depth to their sketches.
Maps often delineate country borders with bold lines to clarify territorial divisions.

senses.definition: to represent or show something or someone by a work of art

examples.x: The painting depicts a serene landscape, with rolling hills and a tranquil river winding through the valley.
In the sculpture, the artist depicts a mother cradling her child, capturing the tenderness of maternal love.
The mural on the city wall depicts scenes from local history, showcasing significant events and figures.
The photograph depicts a bustling city street, capturing the energy and diversity of urban life.
The stained glass window in the church depicts religious scenes from the Bible.

senses.definition: to draw the blueprints and the sketches for a building, machine, structure, etc.

examples.x: The architect drafted the plans for the new office building, incorporating modern design principles and sustainable features.
Engineers were tasked with drafting the schematic diagrams for the construction of the bridge across the river.
Before construction could begin, the team of designers drafted detailed sketches of the amusement park rides.
Before the assembly line could start, engineers had to draft the layout of the factory floor to optimize production efficiency.
The landscape architect drafted blueprints for the botanical garden, ensuring a harmonious blend of flora and pathways.

senses.definition: to carve or cut a design or lettering into a hard surface, such as metal or stone

examples.x: The jeweler engraved the couple's initials on the wedding ring.
The engraver skillfully engraved the family crest on the silver tray.
The sculptor engraved the historical dates on the marble monument.
The monument was engraved with the names of those who had fallen in battle.
The artist engraved intricate patterns onto the silver bracelet, making it a unique piece of art.

senses.definition: to create an artistic piece

examples.x: The sculptor meticulously executed a stunning marble statue.
The musician and composer collaborated to execute a beautiful symphony.
The fashion designer executed a collection of elegant dresses.
The filmmaker worked tirelessly to execute a compelling narrative.

senses.definition: to thoughtfully prepare or create something, paying close attention to its details

examples.x: The scientist spent months formulating a precise hypothesis for the experiment.
To address the issue, the team worked together to formulate a comprehensive action plan.
The chef meticulously formulated a new recipe to enhance the flavors of the dish.
In the meeting, participants collaborated to formulate a strategy for project implementation.
The policy analyst was tasked with formulating recommendations based on thorough research.

senses.definition: to copy or mimic the style, technique, or subject matter of another artist or artwork

examples.x: The student imitated the brushstrokes of Van Gogh to learn his painting technique.
His latest piece imitates the bold colors and geometric patterns of Cubism.
The filmmaker deliberately imitated classic noir cinematography in his latest movie.
She imitated the soft pastels of Monet’s landscapes in her painting.
They created a tribute piece by imitating the surreal imagery of Salvador Dalí.

senses.definition: to make a lasting mark on a surface or material through pressure or contact

examples.x: The author's signature was imprinted on the title page of each limited edition book.
The artist carefully imprinted her fingerprints on the clay sculpture.
The heavy machinery unintentionally imprinted tire tracks on the grass.
The jeweler imprinted a delicate pattern on the metal surface of the pendant.
The company logo was imprinted on every product.

senses.definition: to leave a sign, line, etc. on something

examples.x: Please use a pencil to mark the location where the measurements should be taken.
She carefully marked the important passages in the textbook with colored tabs.
Before cutting the fabric, the tailor used chalk to mark the pattern.
The athlete used a marker to mark the starting line of the race.

senses.definition: to copy the style, technique, or subject matter of another artist or artwork

examples.x: The new artist tried to mimic the brush strokes and color palette of the famous painter.
His latest sculpture was designed to mimic the works of classical Greek artists.
The band's early songs often mimicked the sound of their musical idols.
The chef attempted to mimic the presentation of a Michelin-starred dish for their own menu.
The fashion designer decided to mimic the trends of the 1960s in her latest collection.

senses.definition: to create a smaller representation of something using wood, etc.

examples.x: He modeled a scale replica of the famous landmark using wood and cardboard.
The architect modeled the new building design with foam board and glue.
Can you model a prototype of the new product for the presentation?
The sculptor frequently models miniature versions of famous landmarks.

senses.definition: to come up with or develop something new

examples.x: She originated the concept for the new marketing campaign.
The scientist originated a groundbreaking method for treating the disease.
The groundbreaking theory was originated by a team of young researchers.
Have you ever wondered who originated the design of this iconic building?
The startup originated a creative solution to reduce food waste.

senses.definition: to draw a line or show the outer edge of something

examples.x: The artist outlined the shape of the mountains with a thin brush before adding details to the landscape painting.
To create a map, the cartographer outlined the borders of the country with a bold line.
The children used crayons to outline the shapes of their hands on the paper.
Before cutting the fabric, the seamstress outlined the pattern of the dress on the material with chalk.
The geologist outlined the contours of the rock formation on the map to illustrate its shape and size.

senses.definition: to cause something to remain in its original state without any significant change

examples.x: Historical artifacts are preserved in museums to maintain their original condition.
Conservation efforts work to preserve natural ecosystems and biodiversity.
The library's climate control system helps preserve rare books from damage.
The team is currently preserving the historical documents in a controlled environment.

senses.definition: to make something again or bring it back into existence or imagination

examples.x: She wanted to recreate her grandmother's famous apple pie recipe.
The museum exhibits recreate the atmosphere of different historical periods quite accurately.
The scientists hoped to recreate the conditions of the ancient world in the laboratory.
The artist recreated the scene from memory using vibrant colors.
The author is recreating the magic of their first novel in their latest work, much to the delight of their fans.

senses.definition: to make something, or to bring something into existence
to form a mental image or picture of something

examples.x: Before the competition, athletes often visualize their success.
As she read the novel, she could vividly visualize the scenes described by the author.
Students were encouraged to visualize the historical events they were studying to enhance understanding.
When setting goals, it's helpful to visualize the desired outcomes to increase motivation.
Artists often visualize their creations before putting brush to canvas.
